title: Backpropagation Through a Simple Computational Graph
description: A visual representation of the forward pass and backward pass (backpropagation) for a simple two-layer neural network, showing the flow of data and gradients.
elements: Update Weights and Biases (W = W - α*∂L/∂W)
relationships: Forward Pass: Data flows from Input to Loss calculation
Backward Pass: Gradients (∂L/∂*) are calculated via the chain rule from the Loss back to the Inputs and Parameters
The gradient of a node is computed using the gradient from the next node multiplied by the local derivative
